articles.title: LUMA LAUNCHES CREATIVE AI AGENTS POWERED BY ITS NEW ‘UNIFIED INTELLIGENCE' MODELS
articles.summary: Luma Agents are AI-powered creative assistants that handle end-to-end work across text, image, video, and audio using the company's new Unified Intelligence models. The agents can plan, generate, and refine creative content while coordinating with other AI models, targeting ad agencies, marketing teams, and design studios. Major clients, including Publicis Groupe, Adidas, and Mazda, are already using the platform, which allows users to steer creative direction through conversation rather than manual prompting.

articles.title: AUTODESK'S NEW AI 3D GENERATOR AIMS TO OPEN GAME ART AND 3D PRINTING TO EVERYONE
articles.summary: Wonder 3D is an AI-powered 3D model generator integrated into its cloud-based Flow Studio that creates detailed 3D assets from text prompts and 2D images. The tool aims to democratize 3D creation for game development, 3D printing, and content creation by allowing users without extensive technical skills to generate editable models with geometries and textures. The generated models can be exported as .OBJ files for 3D printing and further editing, potentially accelerating creative workflows across various industries.

articles.title: NOTHING PHONE 4A PRO SIGNALS NEW DESIGN DIRECTION
THE DESIGNER REBUILDING AI INTERFACES FOR HUMANS
articles.summary: AI and tech interfaces fail because they create cognitive friction, forcing users to think too hard about navigation and prompting rather than focusing on their goals. The industry's main challenge is no longer building powerful technology, but designing intuitive interfaces that translate complex systems into effortless user experiences. It's possible to achieve that by replacing problematic interfaces, such as AI prompt boxes, with clear, consistent designs that eliminate guesswork and make advanced technology feel natural to use.

articles.title: FIGMA 2026 DESIGN REPORT: FIVE THINGS EVERY UX DESIGNER SHOULD KNOW
articles.summary: AI is creating a divide in the design profession. Designers who embrace AI tools report 25% higher job satisfaction than those who avoid them. Craft and visual polish are becoming more important as AI makes basic design execution easier, separating designers who focus on quality and emotional impact. While some designers feel optimistic about these changes, others feel anxious. The profession isn't collapsing—it's just becoming more uneven as designers adapt to new tools and workflows.

articles.title: 'DESIGN WITHOUT INTENT IS USELESS': 5 QUESTIONS WITH PAUL WATMOUGH-HALIM
articles.summary: Paul Watmough-Halim, co-founder of Hyperfocus, discusses his design philosophy, emphasizing thoughtful, intentional design over purely visual aesthetics and criticizing careless trends like angled device mockups. He believes the future of design will be shaped by AI and more conversational, personalized technology experiences, while stressing the importance of craft, community support, and the potential for brands to positively influence society.

articles.title: INSTANT PROFESSIONAL DESIGNS WITH AI
articles.summary: Create posters, presentations, and menus by prompting AI. From idea to editable design in seconds.

articles.title: KNOW WHY YOUR AD FAILS BEFORE IT RUNS
articles.summary: HookLens scans your ads frame by frame, analyzes the hook, audio, captions, and body, then tells you exactly what to fix and which markets it'll convert in.

articles.title: CONTENT THAT GETS YOUR VIBE
articles.summary: Picsart is an all-in-one creative platform with a wide selection of free AI design tools. Create content fast with expert-crafted templates, built-in asset libraries, and generative AI tools.

articles.title: THREE WAYS TEAMS ARE BUILDING CONVICTION FASTER WITH FIGMA MAKE
articles.summary: Product managers are increasingly using prototypes rather than traditional Product Requirements Documents (PRDs) to achieve greater clarity faster. Inside Figma Make, teams are pressure-testing assumptions early and building momentum around tangible concepts. This approach helps teams rally around concrete ideas rather than abstract documentation.

articles.title: THE HIDDEN USERS OF YOUR DESIGN SYSTEM AND HOW TO SUPPORT THEM
articles.summary: Design systems impact more than just designers and engineers: they extend to product managers, marketing teams, and QA specialists who use them for roadmapping, brand consistency, and quality verification. These "hidden users" often face barriers such as technical jargon, tool silos, and concerns about creative restrictions that hinder effective adoption. Organizations can maximize their design system's value by creating accessible documentation and addressing the unique needs of cross-functional stakeholders.

articles.title: PRODUCT ETHICS HAVE NEVER MATTERED MORE
articles.summary: The conflict between Anthropic and the US government over AI use in surveillance and autonomous weapons highlights how ethical commitments can be embedded either in product architecture or merely in contracts. When OpenAI accepted a Pentagon deal that Anthropic refused, users reacted by shifting trust and subscriptions, illustrating that people increasingly judge AI companies by whether their values are structurally built into products rather than stated in policy or marketing.

articles.title: PORTFOLIO STORYTELLING: HOW TO PRESENT YOUR WORK LIKE A NARRATIVE
articles.summary: Transforming a designer's portfolio from a static gallery into an emotionally resonant narrative requires structuring projects as case studies with clear context, challenges, and outcomes.

articles.title: EVERYTHING APPLE RELEASED THIS WEEK IS A REMINDER THAT “FROM $599” IS A DESIGN CHOICE
articles.summary: The new MacBook Neo uses an iPhone A18 Pro chip and offers limited specs, serving as Apple's entry into budget territory while maintaining premium positioning.
